DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brett Gibbs, Esq. (415-296-7070) of Weisberg & Miller for Plaintiff.
Deborah Wilson, Esq. (510-466-7100) of McInerney & Dillon for Defendant Thomas Reeves.

RULING - A R G U E D. Parties report the motion to compel is uncontested and the Demurrer is contested as to the negligence claim only. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT THOMAS REEVES' DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the second, third, fourth and fifth causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Wendy Bolker is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to the third and fourth causes of action for breach of express and implied warranties and sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to second and fifth causes of action for negligence and strict liability. Ms. Bolker fails to adequately allege any of the four claims against Mr. Reeves personally. Nor does there appear to be any reasonable possibility that she can state a viable warranty claim against Mr. Reeves personally since the first amended complaint alleges that her agreement was with defendant T R & A, Inc. The court is unable to determine if there is a reasonable possibility that Ms. Bolker may be able to allege a viable claim for negligence or strict liability against Mr. Reeves personally. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Bolker is granted leave to amend to amend. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

T R & A, Inc.'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Petition To Compel Contractual Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Brett Gibbs, Esq. (415-296-7070) of Weisberg & Miller for Plaintiff.
Deborah Wilson, Esq. (510-466-7100) of McInerney & Dillon for Defendant Thomas Reeves.

RULING - A R G U E D. Parties report the motion to compel is uncontested and the Demurrer is contested as to the negligence claim only. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT T R & A, Inc.'S Petition To Compel Contractual Arbitration And To Stay Action Pending Completion Of Arbitration is DENIED. The court exercises its discretion per CCP 1281.2(c) to deny this motion. Defendants KOS & Associates, Inc. and Kenneth O'Sullivan are not parties to the arbitration agreement between plaintiff Wendy Bolker and T R & A. T R & A's billing documents show that T R & A's work related to Ms. Bolker's property is not totally distinct from the work performed by KOS and Mr. O'Sullivan and thus there is a possibility of conflicting rulings if Ms. Bolker's claims against T R &A were arbitrated. Moreover, judicial economy strongly favors resolution of all disputes regarding the claims alleged by Ms. Bolker in a single forum because of common issues of law or fact. In particular, the issue whether T R & A, KOS, and/or Mr. O'Sullivan are responsible for any of Ms. Bolker's claimed damages would likely need to be determined in two fora if this motion were granted.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Apr-06-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT T R & A, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is denied without prejudice to being re-filed to address and submit evidence relevant to the applicability of CCP 1281.2(c). TRA argued that Plaintiff Wendy Bolker's filing of the amended complaint adding Defendants KOS & Associates, Inc. and Kenneth O'Sullivan after TRA filed its motion to compel arbitration prejudiced TRA by depriving it of the ability to properly present supporting evidence as to the applicability of CCP 1281.2(c). Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Kelley DeYoung, CSR No. 6780, email: kelleycsr@aol.com; Dept. 302.
